ticket_title: Adapt platform-apps + metro-api-proxy to Azure Managed Redis Enterprise (single-DB compatibility + Entra MI auth scaffolding)

test_cases.name: TC-1: Metro Proxy starts and reads/writes cache without DB-index error (AccessKey mode) [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Metro Proxy deployed with RTPL-2210-remove-default-database; Redis:Endpoint not set; Caching:RedisConnectionString configured for an Azure Managed Redis Enterprise cluster (DB 0 only). | Test Data: Caching:RedisConnectionString = <Enterprise cluster AccessKey string>, Redis:Endpoint = (not set), env = dev

steps.action: Deploy Metro Proxy RTPL-2210-remove-default-database branch to dev.
Start the service; observe startup logs for Redis connection events.
Trigger a cache read/write via an activation endpoint using IDistributedCache.
Trigger a distributed lock operation via RedisSimpleLockService.
Confirm no SELECT or "ERR DB index is out of range" errors appear in logs.
steps.expected: Service starts cleanly; IDistributedCache and lock operations succeed against DB 0; no DefaultDatabase-related connection errors.

test_cases.name: TC-2: Backend IDistributedCache + FusionCache backplane connect via single multiplexer (AccessKey mode) [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Backend deployed with RTPL-2210-backend-redis-mi-auth; Caching:RedisConnectionString set; Caching:RedisEndpoint not set. | Test Data: Caching:RedisConnectionString = <valid Enterprise string>, Caching:RedisEndpoint = (not set), env = dev

steps.action: Deploy Backend RTPL-2210-backend-redis-mi-auth branch to dev.
Start the service; observe startup logs for Redis connection events.
Trigger a plan/promo fetch to exercise IDistributedCache.
Inspect FusionCache backplane subscription state in logs (expect no subscription errors).
Confirm a single IConnectionMultiplexer is registered in DI (not two separate instances).
steps.expected: IDistributedCache and FusionCache backplane both connect via a single shared multiplexer; no DB-index error; no duplicate connection log entries.

test_cases.name: TC-3: AccessKey mode is selected when Redis:Endpoint is absent (Metro Proxy) [Priority: Medium]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Metro Proxy config does not include Redis:Endpoint. | Test Data: Redis:Endpoint = (absent), Caching:RedisConnectionString = <valid string>

steps.action: Start Metro Proxy without Redis:Endpoint in configuration.
Observe RedisConnectionFactory startup log or trace for mode selection.
Confirm ConfigurationOptions.Parse (AccessKey path) is taken and not BuildManagedIdentityOptions.
Confirm Redis connection succeeds.
steps.expected: AccessKey path is selected; no DefaultAzureCredential or ManagedIdentityCredential invoked; connection to Redis succeeds without auth error.

test_cases.name: TC-4: RedisSmoke harness validates cache and lock round-trips against Enterprise sandbox clusters [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Access to sandbox Enterprise clusters: redis-rt-platform-core-sbx-eastus and redis-rt-platform-core-sbx-westus3; AccessKey credentials available. | Test Data: Target = sbx-eastus and sbx-westus3, auth mode = AccessKey

steps.action: Run RT.Metro.Api.Proxy.RedisSmoke against sbx-eastus with AccessKey credentials.
Confirm cache set/get round-trip succeeds.
Confirm lock acquire/release round-trip succeeds.
Repeat both steps against sbx-westus3.
steps.expected: Both smoke harness runs complete without error across both sandbox clusters; cache and lock operations succeed on DB 0.

test_cases.name: TC-5: Existing dev/qa/stg environments (non-Enterprise Redis) are unaffected after deploy [Priority: High]
test_cases.test_data: Preconditions: Dev environment uses a non-Enterprise Redis cluster with standard AccessKey connection string. | Test Data: Caching:RedisConnectionString = existing dev connection string (non-Enterprise), Redis:Endpoint = (absent), env = dev

steps.action: Deploy both RTPL-2210 branches to dev using existing AccessKey connection strings.
Run a standard new-subscriber activation end-to-end.
Confirm Redis cache and lock operations work as before.
Confirm no regression in activation, refill, or session management flows.
steps.expected: Dev/qa/stg environments continue to operate unchanged; no errors introduced by the new RedisConnectionFactory or the bumped package versions (Azure.Identity 1.17.1, IdentityModel 8.15.0).
